prompts.text: What's something small that's been on your mind lately?
What's one thing you're looking forward to this week?
What song has been stuck in your head recently?
What's a childhood memory you haven't shared with me?
What's something you've changed your mind about recently?
What's a dream you've quietly held onto?
What's one thing about your work I might not understand?
What did you want to be when you were ten?
What part of yourself do you feel I still haven't fully seen?
What's a belief you hold that most people around you don't share?
What's the last thing you searched for on your phone?
What's a show or movie you've been meaning to watch?
What's one thing you ate recently that you really enjoyed?
Who's someone outside our relationship you've been thinking about lately?
prompts.hint: A friend, family member, or colleague

prompts.text: What's something you read or heard recently that stuck with you?
What's something about your daily routine I probably don't notice?
What's a small annoyance from today you haven't mentioned?
What's your current favorite way to unwind?
What's a conversation you had today that you're still thinking about?
What's something about your personality that's shifted in the last few years?
What's one thing you're currently overthinking?
What's a friendship that's been on your mind?
What's a habit you've picked up lately that you're not sure about?
What's something you used to love doing but have quietly stopped?
What role do you play in your friend group that people might not realize?
What's an opinion you hold loosely — you could be convinced otherwise?
What's something about your inner world that would surprise most people?
What's a version of yourself from the past that you miss sometimes?
What's something you carry that you rarely talk about?
prompts.hint: Only share what feels right

prompts.text: What's a question you're afraid to know the answer to?
prompts.hint: About life in general, not necessarily about us

prompts.text: What's a way you've changed that you haven't fully acknowledged to yourself?
What's a story from your life before me that shaped who you are today?
What's a responsibility you carry that weighs on you more than you let on?
What podcast or article have you been into lately?
What's a random fact you learned recently?
What made you smile today?
What's one small thing I did recently that you noticed?
What's something you'd like to do together this weekend?
When did you feel most connected to me this week?
What's one thing I could do that would make tomorrow better?
What's a simple pleasure you wish we did more often?
What's something funny that happened to you recently?
If we had an unexpected free evening, what would you want to do?
What's a small way I made you feel cared for lately?
What's one question you wish I'd ask you more often?
When was the last time you felt truly seen by me?
What's a way I could show up for you that I haven't thought of?
What's one thing that made your day better today?
What's something you'd like us to cook together?
What's a photo on your phone that makes you happy?
What's something you noticed about the world today that you want to share?
What's a song that reminds you of us?
What's one thing you'd love to hear me say more often?
What's a way we've gotten better at being together?
When do you feel closest to me during a normal day?
What's something you want me to know but haven't found the right moment to say?
What's a ritual or routine of ours that means more to you than I might realize?
What's something about us that you want to protect?
What's a moment when you reached for me and I didn't notice?
prompts.hint: This isn't about blame — it's about understanding

prompts.text: What does it feel like when we're really in sync?
What's something you need from this relationship that you've been too proud to ask for?
What's a time you felt lonely even though we were together?
prompts.hint: Share gently — this is about growing closer

prompts.text: What's one thing about me you've never thanked me for?
What's a quality of mine you're grateful for today?
What's something I do that makes your life easier?
When did I make you feel proud recently?
What's one way I've grown since we've been together?
What's a challenge we handled well together?
What's something about our relationship others might envy?
What made you fall for me that still holds true?
What sacrifice have I made that you carry gratitude for?
What's a moment from this past month you'd want to relive?
What's something about us that makes you smile when you think about it?
What's something kind I did this week that you want to acknowledge?
What's one of my quirks that you've grown to love?
What's a recent moment where I made you laugh?
What's something I bring to this relationship that's uniquely mine?
What's a way I've surprised you in a good way?
What's something about the way I parent, work, or show up that you admire?
prompts.hint: Pick whichever applies

prompts.text: What's a hard season we went through where you felt supported by me?
What's something I do for our relationship that often goes unnoticed?
What's a way I've loved you that matched exactly what you needed?
What's something about how we love each other that you'd want to tell your younger self?
What's a moment when you realized this relationship was something rare?
What's a promise I've kept that means more to you than I know?
What's a way I've changed for the better because of us?
What's one thing you want to learn or try someday?
If money weren't an issue, where would you want to travel?
What's a personal goal you're quietly working toward?
What does 'success' mean to you right now?
What's something you want us to experience together someday?
What's a fear you'd like to overcome?
If you could change one thing about how you were raised, what would it be?
What would you do with your life if you knew you couldn't fail?
What's a skill you wish you had more time to develop?
What's a place you've never been but always wanted to visit?
What's a hobby you've been curious about but haven't tried?
If you could take a class in anything, what would it be?
What's a trip you'd want us to take in the next year?
What's something you'd build or create if you had unlimited time?
What does your ideal weekend look like five years from now?
What's a life chapter you feel is still ahead of you?
What kind of older couple do you want us to be?
What's a tradition you'd like us to start?
What's something you want to be remembered for?
What's a dream you've let go of that still matters to you?
What would you need from me to feel safe pursuing your biggest ambition?
What's a part of your identity you want to explore more?
What does growing old together look like in your mind?
What's a conversation about our future that you've been wanting to have?
What's something you'd regret not doing in this lifetime?
What's a creative project you've always wanted to start?
What's one small thing I do that sometimes frustrates you?
prompts.hint: Be honest but kind

prompts.text: Is there something you've been hesitant to bring up?
What's one thing we could do differently in arguments?
What do you need from me when you're stressed?
What's a pattern in our disagreements you wish we could break?
What's something you need to forgive me for that you haven't mentioned?
prompts.hint: Only share if it feels safe

prompts.text: What's one boundary you'd like us to set together?
When we disagree, what's the first emotion you usually feel?
What's something I said recently that landed differently than I intended?
What's a topic we tend to avoid?
What's one household thing that quietly bugs you?
When was the last time you bit your tongue around me?
What's a recurring frustration you'd like us to finally solve?
What's something about how we divide responsibilities that feels uneven?
prompts.hint: This is about finding balance, not keeping score

prompts.text: What's a time I dismissed something that mattered to you?
What's a decision you wish we'd made differently together?
What's something I do when I'm defensive that you've noticed?
How do you know when a small issue is becoming a bigger one between us?
What's something about how your family handled conflict that shows up in us?
What's a hurt you've been holding onto that we haven't fully resolved?
prompts.hint: Share only if you feel ready

prompts.text: What's something you need me to understand about how conflict affects you?
What's a part of our relationship where you feel unheard?
What's a truth about us that's hard to say but important to name?
prompts.hint: Speak from love, not frustration

prompts.text: What's something you wish I understood about your anger or frustration?
What's a surface-level issue that's really about something deeper?
What's something about how we spend money that you'd like to talk through?
Is there anything from recently you'd like a do-over on?
What helps you feel heard when we disagree?
How can I tell when you need space vs. connection?
What's one thing I could say that helps you calm down?
What's a wound from our relationship you're still healing from?
prompts.hint: Share gently — this takes courage

prompts.text: What's one thing I could do after we argue that would help you feel safe again?
What does forgiveness look like to you in a relationship?
What's something I could apologize for that I might not realize affected you?
What's one thing that helps you let go after we've had tension?
When I'm wrong, what's the best way for me to own it?
What's a small gesture that helps us reconnect after distance?
What does it look like when I'm trying to make things right?
What's a repair attempt from me that actually worked?
What's something I do when I'm trying to reconnect that you appreciate?
What's a way I've shown accountability that meant something to you?
What would help you feel safe to be more vulnerable with me?
After a rough patch, what do you need before you can fully move forward?
What's a time we repaired something and came out stronger?
What's an old hurt that still shapes how you show up in disagreements?
prompts.hint: From any relationship, not just ours

prompts.text: What would a real, complete apology from me look like for something that still lingers?
What's something broken between us that you believe we can still fix?
What's a part of our history you need me to acknowledge more?
What would rebuilding trust look like for you if it ever felt shaken?
What's the hardest thing you've had to forgive in this relationship?
prompts.hint: Only share if it feels safe and timely

prompts.text: What's something about your body that you've slowly made peace with over the years?
prompts.hint: Think about how your relationship with your physical self has changed.

prompts.text: What's a money habit you picked up from your family that you're only now noticing?
prompts.hint: Consider how your parents or caregivers talked about or handled money.

prompts.text: What's a song that reminds you of a very specific moment in your life before we met?
What's something you believe about getting older that you didn't believe five years ago?
prompts.hint: This could be something hopeful or something that surprised you.

prompts.text: What's a meal or food that feels like home to you, and why?
What's a friendship you've let go of that you still think about sometimes?
prompts.hint: Not necessarily with regret — maybe just with curiosity.

prompts.text: What's something about the way you grew up that you want to carry forward, and something you want to leave behind?
prompts.hint: Think about traditions, values, or daily rhythms from childhood.

prompts.text: What kind of physical touch do you find most comforting right now? It might be different than it used to be.
prompts.hint: This doesn't have to be about intimacy — it could be a hand on your back or sitting close.

prompts.text: What's something you've been quietly curious about lately — a subject, a skill, a place?
What's one of your daily routines that you'd feel lost without?
What's a part of your spiritual life — or your search for meaning — that you haven't talked about much?
prompts.hint: This could be about faith, nature, philosophy, or something you can't quite name.

prompts.text: What's something at work that's been taking up more of your mental energy than people realize?
What's a fear about money that you carry quietly?
prompts.hint: Think about what financial security or insecurity looks like to you.

prompts.text: What household task do you actually find satisfying, even if you wouldn't admit it?
What's something about the way we laugh together that you'd want to describe to someone?
What's something you've learned about yourself through being in this relationship that surprised you?
prompts.hint: It doesn't have to be dramatic — small realizations count.

prompts.text: What's a place we've been together that you think about more than I realize?
What's a version of your future self that excites you and also scares you a little?
prompts.hint: Think about who you might become in the next decade.

prompts.text: What's an opinion you hold that most people in your life would be surprised by?
What's a habit of mine you've grown fond of, even though it's a little odd?
What does your ideal slow morning look like, in detail?
What's something about parenting — or the idea of parenting — that you think about differently now than you used to?
prompts.hint: This applies whether you have kids, want them, or have decided not to.

prompts.text: What's something you do just for yourself that quietly keeps you whole?
prompts.hint: A ritual, a practice, a pocket of solitude.

prompts.text: What's a small, physical detail about me that you noticed early on and still notice?
What's something you wish you could tell your younger self about what love actually looks like?
prompts.hint: Think about what you expected versus what you've found.

prompts.text: What's something you'd like us to do together this weekend that we haven't done in a while?
I'd like to hear about something that made you smile today, even briefly.
What's a trip or adventure you'd want us to take if we could leave tomorrow?
What's a way I could support your work or ambitions right now that I might not be thinking of?
prompts.hint: This could be something practical or something emotional.

prompts.text: Is there something you've wanted to bring up with me but the moment never felt right?
prompts.hint: It doesn't have to be heavy — sometimes small things just need an opening.

prompts.text: What's a way we could be more playful together? I think we could use more of that.
prompts.hint: Think about inside jokes, games, spontaneous silliness.

prompts.text: What's something about our physical life together that you'd like more of?
prompts.hint: This could be about closeness, touch, intimacy, or just being near each other.

prompts.text: What's a couple we know whose relationship has something you admire? What is it?
When you're having a hard day, what's the most helpful thing I can do — even if it seems too simple?
What's a new tradition or routine you think we should try together?
prompts.hint: Weekly, monthly, seasonal — any cadence.

prompts.text: How do you feel about how we handle money together? Is there anything you'd want to shift?
prompts.hint: Think about spending, saving, sharing, or deciding.

prompts.text: What's something you need from me that you've stopped asking for?
prompts.hint: Sometimes we stop asking because we assume the answer. But I'm here.

prompts.text: What's something fun or silly we used to do that quietly faded away?
Is there a friend of mine you'd like to get to know better, or a friend of yours you wish I knew more?
What would it look like for us to have a really good evening together tonight, with whatever we have?
What's a part of your day where you feel most alone, and could I show up there somehow?
What's something about how we divide up our household that actually works well?
What's something I've been doing well as a partner lately that you'd like me to keep doing?
Is there a way we could take better care of each other's sleep, energy, or health?
What's a moment this week where you felt close to me, even if I didn't realize it?
What's something I did recently that made you feel taken care of?
What's a way I've grown as a person since we've been together that you're proud of?
What's something I bring to our relationship that you know you couldn't get from anyone else?
prompts.hint: Think about what's uniquely mine — a quality, a way of being.

prompts.text: What's something I handle around the house that you'd really notice if I stopped doing it?
What's a hard moment we went through where you were grateful to have me beside you?
What's something I do with our friends or family that you appreciate quietly?
What's a quality of mine that you leaned on during a particularly difficult season?
prompts.hint: Think about patience, humor, steadiness, creativity — whatever comes to mind.

prompts.text: What's a small thing I do that you'd miss if I traveled without you for a week?
What's a way I make you laugh that no one else does?
What's something about how I show up as a partner with money, work, or planning that you value?
When have you seen me be brave in a way that moved you?
prompts.hint: Bravery can be quiet — a conversation you had, a choice you made.

prompts.text: What's something I said to you — maybe a long time ago — that stuck with you in a good way?
What's something about how I parent, or how I think about family, that you admire?
prompts.hint: This applies whether we have kids, want them, or are still deciding.

prompts.text: What's a way I've helped you become more yourself?
What's a recent moment where I handled something well and you wanted to tell me but didn't?
What's something about our daily life together that you secretly love and would protect?
What's a way I make ordinary moments feel a little warmer?
What's something about how I take care of my own health or well-being that you appreciate?
What's something tangible you'd want us to build or create together over the next few years?
prompts.hint: A home, a project, a garden, a business — anything.

prompts.text: What's something you hope we'll still be doing together when we're much older?
If you could design our life five years from now, what does a regular Tuesday look like?
prompts.hint: Think about where you are, what you're doing, who's around.

prompts.text: What's a financial goal you have that feels both exciting and a little daunting?
What kind of home do you imagine us growing old in?
prompts.hint: Think about the space, the neighborhood, the feeling of it.

prompts.text: What's something you'd love to learn or try that you keep putting off?
What's a dream about your career or work life that you haven't fully shared with me?
prompts.hint: Maybe it feels too ambitious, or too different from where you are now.

prompts.text: If we had a year with no obligations, how would you want us to spend it?
What's a tradition you'd like to start — weekly, monthly, or yearly — that's just ours?
What does your ideal relationship with your family look like ten years from now?
prompts.hint: Your family of origin, or the family we're building — or both.

prompts.text: What's a place in the world you've never been that quietly calls to you?
What's something about our social life — friendships, community — that you'd like to see change?
If money weren't a factor, what kind of work would you want to do with your days?
What kind of old person do you want to be? What do you hope stays the same about you?
What's a dream you've let go of that you sometimes wonder about?
prompts.hint: Not with regret, necessarily — just with curiosity about the road not taken.

prompts.text: What's a way you'd like our intimacy to evolve as we grow together?
prompts.hint: This can be physical, emotional, or both.

prompts.text: What's one small change to our daily routine that you think would make both of us happier?
What's a personal goal you have that you'd like my help with — not to fix, just to support?
What's something you'd want to give back to your community or the world, and how could we do it together?
What does retirement — or the later chapter of our life — look like in your imagination?
What's something about how we handle money that you think we should talk about more openly?
What's a surface-level thing we argue about that might actually be about something deeper?
prompts.hint: Think about recurring small disagreements and what might sit underneath them.

prompts.text: When we disagree about money, what do you think each of us is really protecting?
prompts.hint: Financial disagreements often mask deeper values or fears.

prompts.text: What's something about how we split household responsibilities that quietly bothers you?
Is there a pattern in our arguments that you've noticed but we haven't named together?
prompts.hint: Patterns can be about timing, tone, withdrawal, or how we start.

prompts.text: What's something I do when I'm stressed that makes it harder for you to reach me?
When it comes to how we spend our free time, is there a tension we haven't fully worked out?
What's something about our families — yours or mine — that creates friction between us?
prompts.hint: This isn't about blame. It's about understanding the pull we each feel.

prompts.text: What's a topic we tend to avoid because it doesn't feel safe enough to bring up?
prompts.hint: Naming the avoidance is sometimes the first step.

prompts.text: When we disagree, do you feel like I'm trying to understand you or trying to be right?
What's something about our social life or friendships that we see differently?
Is there a way I react to your ambitions or career that doesn't feel supportive, even if I mean it to be?
What's an unspoken rule in our relationship that might not be working anymore?
prompts.hint: Sometimes we follow agreements we never actually made out loud.

prompts.text: When we hit a rough patch, what do you need in the first few minutes — and what makes it worse?
What's a decision we need to make together that we've been quietly postponing?
What's something about how we handle screen time or phones that you'd want to change?
Is there something about our physical intimacy that you wish we could talk about more easily?
prompts.hint: You don't have to have the whole conversation now. Just naming it is enough.

prompts.text: What's the difference between what you need after a disagreement and what I tend to offer?
Is there a way I accidentally dismiss your ideas or preferences without realizing it?
I know things have felt a little off between us. What do you think happened?
prompts.hint: You don't have to have the full story. Just your sense of it.

prompts.text: What's something I could say right now that would help you feel closer to me?
When we go through a hard stretch, what helps you remember that we're on the same team?
Is there something from our last disagreement that still feels unfinished for you?
prompts.hint: Not to reopen the argument — just to make sure nothing is sitting unresolved.

prompts.text: What's a way I've hurt you that I might not fully understand yet?
prompts.hint: I'm asking because I want to understand, not because I want to defend myself.

prompts.text: When you pull away from me, what are you usually feeling underneath?
What's the smallest gesture that could help us reconnect after we've been distant?
prompts.hint: Sometimes the tiniest thing — a touch, a text, a cup of coffee — is the bridge.

prompts.text: Do you feel like I've been present with you this week? Honestly.
What do you need me to understand about how you've been feeling that I might be missing?
I want to make sure you feel safe being honest with me. Do you?
prompts.hint: If the answer is complicated, that's okay. I'd rather know.

prompts.text: What's a time I apologized well and it actually landed for you? What made it work?
Is there a part of you that's been hiding from me lately? Not in a bad way — just quietly retreating.
What would help you trust that when I say I'm sorry, I really mean it?
Can we just sit together for a moment without trying to fix anything?
prompts.hint: Sometimes reconnection starts with shared silence.

prompts.text: When we come back together after being apart — even just a workday — what would make that moment feel better?
What's something I did during a hard time that showed you I was still here?
Is there an old wound between us that healed on the surface but might still be tender underneath?
prompts.hint: I'm not trying to reopen it. I just want to be gentle with it.

prompts.text: What's a quiet hope you have for who we'll become together — not what we'll achieve, but who we'll be?
prompts.hint: Think about the qualities of your partnership, not the milestones.

prompts.text: What's a season of the year that feels most like us, and why?
prompts.hint: Think about the weather, the light, the rituals you share during that time.

prompts.text: When we spend time apart, what's the first thing you look forward to when we're back together?
prompts.hint: It could be something physical, a conversation, or just a feeling.

prompts.text: After our last disagreement, was there a moment where you felt us start to come back together? What did that feel like?
prompts.hint: Try to name the turning point, even if it was small.

prompts.text: What does closeness feel like to you on a quiet morning when neither of us has anywhere to be?
prompts.hint: Think about what you notice — touch, silence, routine.

prompts.text: What's one small thing I did this week that helped you get through a hard day?
prompts.hint: Even something I might not have noticed I was doing.

prompts.text: If we could take a month off from everything and go anywhere, where would you want to wake up?
prompts.hint: Don't worry about logistics. Just picture the morning.

prompts.text: When the holidays come around, is there a tradition from your family you wish we carried into ours?
prompts.hint: It could be something big or something no one else would notice.

prompts.text: What's a night out we had together that still makes you smile when you think about it?
prompts.hint: It doesn't have to be fancy. Sometimes the best ones aren't.

prompts.text: Is there something you've been carrying from work this week that you haven't said out loud yet?
prompts.hint: It doesn't need to be a crisis. Sometimes the weight is in the small stuff.

prompts.text: What's something silly about us that you hope never changes?
prompts.hint: An inside joke, a weird habit, a thing only we would understand.

prompts.text: When you think about the next big chapter of our life together, what do you picture?
prompts.hint: It could be a change you're hoping for or one you sense is coming.

prompts.text: At the end of a long day, what's the version of winding down together that feels best to you?
prompts.hint: Think about what helps you actually let go of the day.

prompts.text: Is there a way I reach for you — physically or emotionally — that always makes you feel safe?
What's a place from our past — an apartment, a restaurant, a stretch of road — that you'd want to revisit together?
prompts.hint: Think about why that place still holds something for you.

prompts.text: When we argue, what's the thing I could say or do afterward that would help you feel like we're okay again?
prompts.hint: This isn't about fixing the issue. It's about reconnecting.

prompts.text: What's one thing on your bucket list you've never told me about?
prompts.hint: It can be wild or quiet. No judgment here.

prompts.text: When you're away from me for a few days, what do you miss that might surprise me?
prompts.hint: Often it's not the obvious things.

prompts.text: What's a lazy Sunday morning that stands out in your memory — one that felt like exactly what you needed?
Is there something about the way we handle stress together that you think we've gotten better at over time?
prompts.hint: It might be something subtle — a pattern you've noticed shifting.

prompts.text: If you had to describe the feeling of falling asleep next to me in one sentence, what would it be?
When we look back at this year together, what moment do you think we'll remember most?
prompts.hint: It doesn't have to be a big event. Sometimes the quiet ones stick.

prompts.text: What's a dream you had for us early on that actually came true, even if it looks different than you imagined?
What's the best meal we've ever shared? Not the fanciest — the one that felt the most right.
When something big changes in our life — a move, a loss, a new beginning — what do you need from me in those first few days?
prompts.hint: Think about past transitions and what helped or what you wished for.

prompts.text: What's a small morning ritual of yours that you'd want me to understand better?
prompts.hint: The things we do on autopilot often matter more than we realize.

prompts.text: Is there a fight we had a while back that taught you something important about us?
prompts.hint: You don't need to rehash it. Just name what you took from it.

prompts.text: What's something you'd love for us to try together this summer that we've never done?
After we go out together — dinner, a movie, a walk — what part of the evening do you replay in your mind?
When you're stressed during the week, do you want me to help fix things, or just sit with you in it?
prompts.hint: There's no wrong answer. And it might change day to day.

prompts.text: What's a song that reminds you of a specific moment in our relationship?
Is there something about the way we were in the beginning that you'd like to bring back, even just a little?
prompts.hint: Not because things are worse now. Just because some things are worth revisiting.

prompts.text: What's a winter evening ritual you'd love for us to have?
prompts.hint: Think about warmth, quiet, comfort.

prompts.text: When I come home after being apart for a while, what do you notice first?
What's a hard season we went through that you're quietly proud we survived together?
If we could design the perfect nothing-to-do day together, what would yours look like from morning to night?
Is there something I do when we're getting ready for bed that makes you feel connected to me?
prompts.hint: Sometimes the smallest end-of-day gestures carry the most weight.

prompts.text: What's a fear about the future that you carry quietly, and what would help you feel less alone with it?
What's the funniest misunderstanding we've ever had?
When we're driving somewhere together with no real agenda, what does that time feel like for you?
What's something about our life together that you think other people don't see, but means a lot to you?
If we were to write a letter to ourselves five years from now, what would you want it to say about where we are today?
On the mornings when you wake up before me, what goes through your mind?
What's a holiday memory from your childhood that you'd want our future to echo somehow?
prompts.hint: It doesn't have to be a big tradition. Sometimes it's a feeling more than an event.

prompts.text: After a disagreement, do you need space first, or closeness? And has that changed over time?
What's a trip we haven't taken yet that you think would be good for us — not just fun, but actually good for us?
What's a way you've grown since we've been together that you don't think you could have done alone?
When you picture us at seventy, what are we doing on a regular Tuesday?
What's something about the way we touch — a hand on the back, the way we sit on the couch — that matters to you more than I might realize?
What's the best text I ever sent you?
When we go through a big life change together, what's the thing I do that steadies you?
prompts.hint: Think about a specific transition and what helped most.

prompts.text: Is there a way the workweek pulls us apart that we could gently push back against?
prompts.hint: This isn't about blame. It's about noticing a pattern together.

prompts.text: What's a sound in our home that feels like comfort to you?
If tonight were a perfect end to the day, what would the last hour look like?
What's a conversation we had once that changed how you saw me?
When the seasons shift and the light changes, does it stir anything in you about where we are in life?
What's a random weeknight that turned into something unexpectedly good between us?
Is there something you wish I'd ask you about more often?
What's a loss or ending we've gone through together that made us closer, even if it was painful?
prompts.hint: You don't have to go deep into the pain. Just name what it built between you.

prompts.text: What's a skill or hobby you'd love for us to learn together, just for the fun of being beginners?
When I'm far away, what's the best way for me to make you feel like I'm still right there?
prompts.hint: A call, a text, a photo, silence — everyone's different.

prompts.text: What does it feel like when we repair something between us — not during, but after, when it's actually better?
What's a photo of us that captures something true about who we are together?
What's something you've been wanting to say yes to but haven't yet?
prompts.hint: In our relationship or in life. Either counts.

prompts.text: When Friday evening arrives, what's the shift you feel? And is there something I could do to make that transition better?
What's a time I showed up for you that you still think about?
If you had to pick one word for what our relationship feels like right now — not good or bad, just honest — what would it be?
What's something about the way we celebrate our anniversaries that you'd want to keep, and something you'd change?
What's your favorite thing about the way our home smells?
Is there a version of intimacy between us that has nothing to do with the physical — something that feels just as close?
When was the last time you laughed really hard because of something I did?
What's a part of your week that feels heavy, and how could I make it a little lighter without you having to ask?
What's a 'remember when' moment that always brings you back to how you felt about us early on?
What's something we used to fight about that we've quietly figured out? How did that happen?
If you could bottle the feeling of our best day together and open it whenever you needed to, which day would you choose?
What's something new about your body, your health, or the way you feel in it that I should know about?
prompts.hint: This is about being seen, not about fixing anything.

prompts.text: What's a someday plan — something we've said 'we should do that' about — that you actually want to make real?
When tension rises between us, what's the thing you most want me to understand about what's happening inside you?
What's a spring or summer feeling — the light, the warmth, the longer days — that makes you think of us?
What's one thing you want me to know before we fall asleep tonight?
What's the quietest way I've ever told you I love you?
prompts.hint: Not with words. The other ways.
